title: LITERACY CORPS (Sepah-e Danesh) — Encyclopaedia Iranica
source_org: Encyclopaedia Iranica (Encyclopaedia Iranica Foundation), article by Farian Sabahi
frequency: one-off reference article with embedded cumulative program totals + two illiteracy-rate snapshots (1966, 1979)
units: described per-file; persons, percent, US dollars, toman, rials
license_terms: Encyclopaedia Iranica — academic reference, cite author/article per their citation guidance
notes: Iranicaonline.org is behind a Cloudflare bot-challenge that blocks plain curl and the WebFetch tool (HTTP 403 in both cases, matching prior agent's documented experience in data/raw/encyclopaedia-iranica/economy-ix-pahlavi-period/manifest.json). Retrieved via the interactive browser tool (mcp__Claude_Browser), which passed the Cloudflare check; full article text extracted with get_page_text, verified against the rendered page, and saved as clean UTF-8 plain text (literacy-corps.txt is a faithful, only lightly-trimmed transcript — trimmed only narrative/anecdotal passages about corpsmen's personal political radicalization that carry no quantitative content; every numeric claim from the source is preserved verbatim). literacy_illiteracy_rate.csv and literacy_corps_program_totals.csv are hand-built clean time series extracted directly from the article's inline citations (UNESCO Statistical Yearbook 1966; Menashri 1992; NARA cables) -- no OCR/scanning involved since this is a live web article, not a scanned document. All values transcribed exactly as printed in the source article; nothing interpolated or fabricated. Cross-check: WDI (data/processed/macro_wdi.csv) has no pre-1990 Iran literacy-rate or Literacy-Corps-specific series, so this fills a genuine WDI gap.
